title: Light – Reflection and Refraction
subject: science
audioScript: Good morning, everyone. Today we're opening Light – Reflection and Refraction. Forget the heavy textbook tone for a moment — think of this chapter as a story about Spherical mirrors, Spherical lenses, and Mirror formula. I'll use everyday examples first, then we'll connect them to what CBSE expects in the exam.

segments.teleprompter: Good morning, everyone. Today we're opening Light – Reflection and Refraction. Forget the heavy textbook tone for a moment — think of this chapter as a story about Spherical mirrors, Spherical lenses, and Mirror formula. Imagine I'm sitting beside you before the board exam. Here's Light – Reflection and Refraction the way I'd explain it to a student who said 'I read it but didn't get it.' Slow pace, simple words, one idea at a time.

sections.title: 1. Concept Overview
sections.body:
| 
                CBSE VISIONके.मा.शि.बो. संदृश्य
                   The CBSE envisions a robust, vibrant and holistic school education that will engender excellence in every sphere of human endeavour. The Board is committed to provide quality education to promote
                    intellectual, social and cultural vivacity among its learners. It works towards evolving a learning process and environment, which empowers the future citizens to become global leaders in the
                    emerging knowledge society. The Board advocates Continuous and Comprehensive Evaluation with an emphasis on holistic development of learners. The Board commits itself to providing a stress-free
                    learning environment that will develop competent, confident and enterprising citizens who will promote harmony and peace.केन्द्रीय माध्यमिक शिक्षा बोर्ड विद्यालयों में ऐसी सुदृढ़, जीवंत व सम्पूर्ण शिक्षा की कल्पना करता है जो मानवीय प्रयास के प्रत्येक क्षेत्र में उत्कृष्टता उत्पन्न करवाए। बोर्ड छात्र /छात्राओं को ऐसी शिक्षा उपलब्ध करवाने हेतु प्रतिबद्ध है जो उनमें बौद्धिक, सामाजिक व सांस्कृतिक सजीवता का प्रसार कर सके। बोर्ड ऐसी अधिगम प्रक्रिया व वातावरण उत्पन्न करवाने की दिशा में प्रय…
**Crawled sources (internet):**
- [CBSE | Central Board of Secondary Education](https://cbseacademic.nic.in/)
- [CBSE | Central Board of Secondary Education](https://cbseacademic.nic.in/?ref=w3use)

sections.body:

- learning environment that will develop competent, confident and enterprising citizens who will promote harmony and peace.केन्द्रीय माध्यमिक शिक्षा बोर्ड विद्यालयों में ऐसी सुदृढ़, जीवंत व सम्पूर्ण शिक्षा की कल्पना करता है जो मानवीय प्रयास के प्रत्येक क्षेत्र में उत्कृष्टता उत्पन्न करवाए। बोर्ड छात्र /छात्राओं को ऐसी शिक्षा उपलब्ध करवाने हेतु प्रतिबद्ध है जो उनमें बौद्धिक, सामाजिक व सांस्कृतिक सजीवता का प्रसार कर सके। बोर्ड ऐसी अधिगम प्रक्रिया व वातावरण उत्पन्न करवाने की दिशा में प्रयासरत है जो भावी नागरिकों को उभरते हुए ज्ञानाधारित समाज में सार्वभौमिक नेता बनने में सक्षम कर सके। छात्र /छात्राओं के सम्पूर्ण विकास हेतु बोर्ड सतत् एवं व्यापक मूल्यांकन का समर्थन करता है। बोर्ड ऐसा तनाव रहित अधिगम वातावरण उपलब्ध करवाने हेतु प्रतिबद्घ है जिससे छात्र/ छात्राएं कार्यसक्षम, आत्मविश्वासी व उद्यमशील नागरिक बनें जो शांति व सद्भाव का प्रसार करें। About Academics Unitशैक्षणिक इकाई के बारे मेंThe goal of the Academic, Training, Innovation and Research unit of Central Board of Secondary Education is to achieve academic excellence by conceptualising policies and their operational planning to

sections.title: 8. Practice Questions
sections.body:
_Class 10 CBSE practice for **Light – Reflection and Refraction**._

**Syllabus-based cbse practice set** _(auto-generated for review — Phase-II ingest)_

- **SHORT (2 marks)** — Explain **Spherical mirrors** in 2–3 sentences. Use one example from Light – Reflection and Refraction.
- **MCQ (1 mark)** — MCQ: Which statement about **Spherical mirrors** is most accurate (Class 10 CBSE)? Review your notes, then justify your choice.
- **SHORT (2 marks)** — Explain **Spherical lenses** in 2–3 sentences. Use one example from Light – Reflection and Refraction.
- **MCQ (1 mark)** — MCQ: Which statement about **Spherical lenses** is most accurate (Class 10 CBSE)? Review your notes, then justify your choice.
- **SHORT (2 marks)** — Explain **Mirror formula** in 2–3 sentences. Use one example from Light – Reflection and Refraction.
- **MCQ (1 mark)** — MCQ: Which statement about **Mirror formula** is most accurate (Class 10 CBSE)? Review your notes, then justify your choice.
- **SHORT (2 marks)** — Explain **Lens formula** in 2–3 sentences. Use one example from Light – Reflection and Refraction.
- **MCQ (1 mark)** — MCQ: Which statement about **Lens formula** is most accurate (Class 10 CBSE)? Review your notes, then justify your choice.
- **SHORT (2 marks)** — Explain **Magnification** in 2–3 sentences. Use one example from Light – Reflection and Refraction.
- **MCQ (1 mark)** — MCQ: Which statement about **Magnification** is most accurate (Class 10 CBSE)? Review your notes, then justify your choice.
- **SHORT (3 marks)** — Using **1/f = 1/v + 1/u**, solve a standard board-style problem from Light – Reflection and Refraction. Show all working steps.
- **SHORT (3 marks)** — Using **m = h'/h = -v/u**, solve a standard board-style problem from Light – Reflection and Refraction. Show all working steps.
